mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following positive month-over-month RSAFS growth are positive in fewer than 55% of observations, or the mean return is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in 50% or more of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the mean return under the trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and an increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a broad-based labor market recovery that boosts consumer income expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; neither series alone captures the strength.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling UNRATE and rising PAYEMS does not outperform PAYEMS alone in predicting positive 20-day returns, or forward returns are positive in fewer than 60% of joint-signal months.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because it confirms that restrictive policy is triggering labor market deterioration.
failure_condition: In months occurring during monetary tightening cycles, rising UNRATE is not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 1,300 thousand units signal robust residential investment and upstream commodity demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not positive in more than 60% of months when HOUST exceeds 1,300, or the mean excess return is not significantly greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of an elevated federal funds rate above 4.0% (FEDFUNDS) and a month-over-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) produces sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days, as tight monetary policy coincides with weakening real supply.
failure_condition: The joint condition does not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered periods, or a linear model with an interaction term does not improve prediction accuracy over FEDFUNDS alone (incremental R-squared < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation measured by a month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL exceeding 0.3% compresses real corporate earnings and raises the probability of additional monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in fewer than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL accelerates more than 0.3%, or the mean return under the trigger is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month expansion in the industrial production index (INDPRO) signals improving capacity utilization and positive operating leverage for manufacturing firms, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month INDPRO growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of observations, or the mean return is not statistically greater than zero.
